evidence: src/integrations/supabase/client.ts keeps a deprecated compatibility export (`supabase`) implemented via `createSupabaseCompatibilityProxy()`, which preserves legacy access patterns while bypassing explicit bootstrap boundaries (`getSupabaseClient`/`initializeSupabase`).
src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx is a deprecated compatibility barrel that re-exports from `@/shared/hooks/mobile/*`, so old and new module paths coexist and keep coupling to legacy entry points.

src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx imports `cn` via `@/shared/lib/utils`, while src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx and src/shared/components/ui/label.tsx import `cn` from `@/shared/components/ui/cn`, indicating split dependency paths for the same UI primitive concern.

src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/LightboxShell.tsx mixes responsibilities: modal shell rendering, browser-specific viewport repair logic, body scroll locking policy, task-pane offset layout logic, and interaction wiring.

src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts combines cross-cutting concerns (event-bus subscription, navigation orchestration, lightbox lifecycle, shot filter control, task details modal activation) inside one hook, creating a broad ownership surface.

src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/components/ShotActions.tsx accepts grouped state/callback objects but still centralizes multiple UI action policies (shot selection, add-with-position, add-without-position, quick-create success navigation), making role boundaries less obvious at the component layer.

`useButtonGroupProps` in `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useButtonGroupProps.ts` rebuilds and returns the same nested shape it receives, then that bag is re-wrapped again in `useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` and `ImageLightbox.tsx` before reaching `LightboxLayout.tsx`.

`useInlineEditState` and `useImageEditValue` split one large state contract into many builder helpers but still pass through a single very wide parameter object with minimal transformation (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts`, `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts`).
`useImageEditValue` memoizes on `[params]`, but `useInlineEditState` constructs a fresh params object per render, so the memoized abstraction does not reduce recomputation.

Boundary translation logic (UI settings -> task request payload) is duplicated in separate modules (`segmentSettingsUtils` and `generateVideoService`) instead of a single seam, increasing drift risk at integration points.

`generationTaskRepository` preserves mapping status (`ok`, `query_failed`, `scope_mismatch`, etc.) but `enhanceGenerationsWithTaskData` discards status and only forwards `taskId/taskData`, weakening downstream error-aware orchestration.
`prefetchAdjacentGenerationPages` serially awaits next then previous prefetch, making a symmetric boundary operation unnecessarily sequential.

The legacy structure-field cleanup list is duplicated in two task payload builders (`LEGACY_STRUCTURE_PARAMS` in `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts` and `legacyStructureParams` in `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts`) with identical delete loops.

Large object-construction blocks in task payload builders include many conditional spreads and manual deletes, increasing local edit risk and making invariants harder to verify (`src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts`).

Shot image upload/drop contracts diverge between shared handler types and component props: `ImageUploadHandler` uses a DOM change event while `ShotUploadProps.onImageUpload` takes `File[]`, and both are presented as part of the same shared handler surface.

Travel task payload construction is split across multiple public helpers (`buildTaskParams`, `buildTravelRequestBody`, `buildTravelRequestBodyV2`) with overlapping but non-identical field conventions and fallback behavior.
Edge request body parsing exposes two parallel response shapes (`OperationResult` and legacy `{ body, errorResponse }` adapter), which keeps multiple caller protocols alive.

src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/utils/image-utils.ts contains long explanatory comment blocks (lines 8-20) that restate type fields and call-site behavior instead of encoding that intent via types/tests.

src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/utils/image-utils.ts exports a pass-through wrapper getAspectRatioStyle() (lines 58-60) that only forwards to getProjectAspectRatioStyle with no translation/guarding.
src/shared/hooks/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx has a large usage tutorial block (lines 5-26) for a small hook, increasing maintenance noise in production code.

Multiple compatibility shims remain active and export old paths: src/shared/components/BatchDropZone.tsx, src/shared/components/ConfirmDialog.tsx, src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/utils/download.ts, src/shared/hooks/useBatchImageLoading.ts, src/shared/hooks/useClickRipple.ts, src/shared/hooks/useGalleryPageState.ts, src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx, src/shared/lib/NetworkStatusManager.ts, src/shared/lib/aspectRatios.ts, src/shared/lib/autoplayMonitor.ts, src/shared/lib/clientThumbnailGenerator.ts, src/shared/lib/deepEqual.ts.
src/integrations/supabase/client.ts keeps both the new API (initializeSupabase/getSupabaseClient) and deprecated lazy proxy export supabase (lines 85-98), prolonging dual-path usage.

src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts preserves a broad deprecated payload surface (motion_strength, structure_video_* fields, use_uni3c) alongside newer structure_guidance contracts.

Objective structure signal: `src/shared/lib/` has 79 production files (>10 threshold) with mixed concerns (UI helpers, Supabase helpers, task utilities, media utilities), indicating low cohesion for a single package bucket.
`src/shared/lib/utils.ts` is a thin generic barrel (2 re-exports) yet has high fan-in (101 importers), making it a navigational choke point with ambiguous ownership.

Objective structure signal: both `src/types/` (12 files, avg fan-in 22.3) and `src/domains/generation/types/` (8 files) exist for generation/shot contracts, showing package boundary overlap rather than one domain-owned type home.

Objective structure signal: root config files are all peripheral (`fan_in < 5`), while package-level organization burden sits in large shared directories; this increases dependency on broad shared buckets instead of focused domain packages.

Timeline flow is split across both `Timeline.tsx` and `useTimelineOrchestrator.ts`, but `Timeline.tsx` still owns data fetching (`useGetTask`), preloading, event listeners, adapter error handling, and lightbox composition in one component (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline.tsx:265-533`).

`useSharedLightboxState` uses a very wide composed props contract and returns a large multi-section state object spanning variants/navigation/shots/layout/button wiring (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useSharedLightboxState.ts:53-253`), while `ImageLightbox.tsx` adds additional environment/state assembly before passing through (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx:95-193`).

LoRA management behavior is duplicated in two hooks (`useLoraManager` and `useLoraSync`) including add/remove/strength updates, save/load persistence, trigger-word handling, and header-action rendering (`src/shared/hooks/useLoraManager.tsx:151-459`, `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/hooks/useLoraSync.tsx:111-260`).

Timeline image-selection/filtering semantics are implemented in multiple places (`useTimelineCore` positioned item filter vs `pairConfig` image shot-generation filter), increasing drift risk in what counts as a timeline image (`src/shared/hooks/useTimelineCore.ts:159-183`, `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideo/pairConfig.ts:24-37`).

Task modules bundle multiple layers in single files: validation, defaults, model selection, payload assembly, and task creation; `individualTravelSegment.ts` additionally performs child-generation lookup and legacy field cleanup in the same module (`src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts:183-529`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts:92-709`).

UI primitive siblings use different formatting conventions: `src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx`, `src/shared/components/ui/label.tsx`, `src/shared/components/ui/toast.tsx`, and `src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx` use double-quote/no-semicolon style, while `src/shared/components/ui/touchableTooltip.tsx` uses single-quote/semicolon style.

Utility import conventions drift inside the same UI layer: `button.tsx` imports `cn` from `@/shared/lib/utils`, while `label.tsx`/`toast.tsx`/`tooltip.tsx` import `cn` from `@/shared/components/ui/cn`.

`useAIInteractionService` provides throw/safe variants and swallows failures to `null`/`[]` (`generateSummarySafe`, `generatePromptsSafe`) while sibling hooks throw explicit `Error` on data fetch failures (`src/features/billing/hooks/useCredits.ts`).

React Query retry policy checks only message substrings ('unauthorized'/'forbidden') (`src/app/providers/queryClient.ts`), but auth helpers throw context-prefixed errors ('Not authenticated', 'Failed to get session') (`src/integrations/supabase/auth/ensureAuthenticatedSession.ts`), creating inconsistent retry behavior.

Several catches intentionally suppress failures (`src/app/hooks/useOnboardingFlow.ts` product-tour preload catch; `src/features/gallery/hooks/useGalleryPageState.ts` converts mutation failures to `false`).
Route redirection uses a localStorage token presence heuristic in `src/app/routes.tsx` (`hasStoredSessionToken`) before routing users to protected tool paths.

Auth-sensitive operations in `src/pages/Home/hooks/auth/useAuthReferralFinalize.ts` and `src/pages/Home/hooks/useDiscordSignIn.ts` rely on Supabase auth/RPC execution via `runAuthSupabaseOperation`, creating two different auth-source patterns (storage hint vs authenticated operation).
`supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts` enforces explicit service-role auth via `authenticateRequest`, showing stricter server-boundary checks than client-side route prechecks.

findings.summary: Long-lived compatibility facades keep old access paths active and weaken architectural boundaries.
evidence: Supabase client exports deprecated `supabase` proxy with a removal target but still supports implicit lazy runtime initialization through proxy traps.
`useMobile.tsx` is explicitly marked deprecated yet remains an active compatibility surface re-exporting the newer mobile modules.
Active feature code (e.g., `CreativePartnerPane`) still imports `useIsMobile` from the deprecated surface, showing migration not fully enforced.
findings.suggestion: Enforce a migration cutoff by replacing remaining imports to direct mobile module paths, then remove deprecated barrels/proxy exports behind a tracked codemod + CI lint rule that forbids legacy import specifiers.

findings.summary: UI primitives depend on duplicated utility entrypoints, creating inconsistent module boundaries.
evidence: `button.tsx` imports `cn` from `@/shared/lib/utils` (a broad shared barrel).
`tooltip.tsx` and `label.tsx` import `cn` from `@/shared/components/ui/cn` (UI-local path).
`src/shared/lib/utils.ts` is a tiny re-export barrel, but UI components are split between lib-level and UI-level utility entrypoints.
findings.suggestion: Choose a single canonical UI classnames entrypoint and migrate all UI primitives to it; keep `shared/lib/utils` focused on non-UI helpers or decompose it to avoid cross-layer ambiguity.

findings.summary: Key shared modules blend orchestration, platform quirks, and feature behavior, reducing ownership clarity.
evidence: `LightboxShell` contains dialog rendering plus iOS viewport repair behavior, scroll locking, overlay geometry for tasks pane, and event handling glue.
`useMediaGalleryHandlers` handles event-bus integration (`useAppEventListener`), shot navigation, filter state, lightbox closure, and task-details modal transitions.
`useMobileInteractions` owns gesture recognition timing and global window/document listeners for UI dismissal, further distributing interaction policy across multiple shared hooks.
findings.suggestion: Extract platform/browser-behavior policies into dedicated infrastructure hooks (e.g., viewport lock/repair), and split gallery orchestration into smaller domain-specific hooks (navigation, event-bus bridge, task-details flow) with a thin composition layer.

findings.summary: Lightbox button-group abstractions add indirection but mostly forward unchanged data.
evidence: `useButtonGroupProps` reconstructs `topRight/bottomLeft/bottomRight` from the same input fields without validation/translation.
Both image and video render models then create another `buttonGroupProps` object and pass it into `LightboxLayout`, which immediately spreads the groups into controls.
findings.suggestion: Remove `useButtonGroupProps` and pass stable typed control props directly from state hooks to `LightboxLayout` (or keep one boundary mapper in a single place) to cut one wrapper layer and reduce prop-churn points.

findings.summary: Inline edit state uses an oversized parameter contract that mostly re-exposes values.
evidence: `UseImageEditValueParams` contains dozens of fields, then `buildModeState/buildCanvasAndAnnotationState/buildRepositionAndPanelState/buildFormAndGenerationState` mostly mirror those fields into output.
`useImageEditValue` depends on `[params]`, but `buildImageEditValueParams(...)` creates a fresh object in `useInlineEditState`, so memoization does not provide meaningful stability.
findings.suggestion: Collapse this into smaller domain slices (for example `mode`, `canvas`, `generation`) with explicit sub-objects or context providers; memoize each slice on primitive deps and compose at the boundary instead of one monolithic params bag.

findings.summary: Image and video lightboxes duplicate boundary wiring into `useLightboxWorkflowProps`.
evidence: Both files construct nearly identical nested objects for `panel`, `shotWorkflow`, `actions`, and `buttonGroupProps` with slight variations.
This seam duplication increases divergence risk when adding or changing workflow props because both callsites must stay in lockstep.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared workflow-props assembler (with typed overrides for image/video differences) and reuse it in both render paths.

findings.summary: Legacy structure-guidance key cleanup is duplicated in two payload builders.
evidence: Both files maintain nearly identical arrays of legacy structure param names and run delete loops before returning payloads.
Any future key additions/removals must be synchronized manually, which is error-prone and easy to drift.
findings.suggestion: Move legacy-structure cleanup into one shared helper (for example `stripLegacyStructureParams(record)`) and reuse it across both builders.

findings.summary: Shared shot-image handler API exposes conflicting upload signatures across adjacent types.
evidence: `ImageUploadHandler` is defined as `(event: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => Promise<void>` in shared handler types.
`ShotUploadProps.onImageUpload` is separately defined as `(files: File[]) => Promise<void>`, while the same file also imports shared handler types for related operations.
This forces consumers/adapters to special-case one operation with a different shape inside the same handler family.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on one upload contract at the boundary (prefer `File[]` for non-UI domain handlers), update `imageHandlers.ts` and manager prop groups accordingly, and keep DOM event parsing at component edge only.

findings.summary: Task payload mapping is duplicated across modules, creating drift-prone integration seams.
evidence: `buildTaskParams` assembles snake_case task payload fields and motion fields from segment settings/context.
`generateVideoService` independently normalizes request input and rebuilds a largely overlapping payload (`buildTravelRequestBodyV2`) including motion/prompt/model fields and per-pair overrides.
Both modules perform boundary translation and conditional field inclusion, but with different composition paths and defaults.
findings.suggestion: Extract a single shared request-builder seam for travel-between-images payloads (with explicit mode-specific extensions), then have both modules call it so field naming/default rules are owned in one place.

findings.summary: Generate-video request builder keeps legacy and v2 input protocols in one overloaded API surface.
evidence: `TravelRequestBodyInput` accepts both grouped params (`motionParams/generationParams/seedParams`) and many legacy flat fields, then `normalizeTravelRequestBodyInput` reconciles both.

The same module publicly exports multiple internal builders (`_buildTravelRequestBody`, `_buildTravelRequestBodyV2`, and additional internals), widening the callable surface beyond a single canonical contract.
Parallel payload construction exists in `segmentSettingsUtils`, compounding protocol ambiguity for callers.
findings.suggestion: Freeze one canonical request DTO (v2), move legacy flattening into a temporary adapter with deprecation boundary, and narrow exports to a minimal stable API.

findings.summary: Edge request parsing utilities expose two incompatible caller protocols for the same operation.
evidence: `parseJsonBody/parseJsonBodyStrict` return `OperationResult<Record<string, unknown>>`.
`parseJsonBodyStrictResponse` returns legacy `{ body?: ..., errorResponse?: Response }` and is explicitly marked as an adapter.
Shared edge helpers like `rateLimitResponse` and `edgeErrorResponse` promote direct response construction, so mixed parse protocols increase handler-level branching patterns.
findings.suggestion: Migrate handlers to `OperationResult` only, keep the legacy adapter internal/private, and remove it after callsite migration to enforce one edge-handler parsing contract.

findings.summary: Task-mapping status is lost when data crosses from repository to generation enhancement layer.
evidence: Repository APIs return `GenerationTaskMapping` with explicit statuses (`query_failed`, `scope_mismatch`, etc.).
Cache preloading stores `{ taskId, status }`, but `enhanceGenerationsWithTaskData` emits only `taskId` and `taskData` on generation rows.
Downstream callers cannot distinguish missing tasks from query/scope failures at the integration seam.
findings.suggestion: Propagate mapping status in the enhanced generation output (or a companion field) so UI/orchestrators can react deterministically to failure modes.

findings.summary: Adjacent page prefetch orchestration is serialized despite independent operations.
evidence: `prefetchAdjacentGenerationPages` awaits next-page prefetch before starting previous-page prefetch.
Both prefetches are independent reads with separate priorities and identical error handling shape.
The surrounding cache/invalidation infrastructure is already asynchronous and batched, so this serialized seam adds avoidable latency.
findings.suggestion: Run previous/next prefetches in parallel (e.g., `Promise.allSettled`) while preserving priority arguments and current cleanup behavior.

findings.summary: Legacy import-path shims are still widespread, extending dual API surfaces.
evidence: Each file is a deprecated compatibility re-export with comments directing callers to new module ownership.
The volume of active shim files in this batch indicates migration is not bounded to isolated holdouts.
findings.suggestion: Add a migration cutoff plan: (1) codemod remaining imports to canonical targets, (2) block new imports of shim paths via ESLint no-restricted-imports, and (3) delete shim files in one removal PR once import count reaches zero.

findings.summary: Deprecated Supabase proxy keeps runtime dual-path behavior alive.
evidence: src/integrations/supabase/client.ts exports deprecated `supabase` proxy (lines 85-98) while also exposing `getSupabaseClient()` and `initializeSupabase()`.
Proxy traps dynamically forward property reads/writes (lines 55-82), so legacy usage remains hard to statically detect and easy to keep shipping.
Fallback initialization/error path logs runtime recovery (lines 25-51), confirming old and new initialization modes still coexist.
findings.suggestion: Replace `supabase` proxy with a temporary throwing getter in development once migration starts, migrate all imports to `getSupabaseClient()`, then remove proxy export and fallback lazy-init path together.

findings.summary: Several modules carry formulaic explanatory noise and zero-value forwarding wrappers.
evidence: image-utils.ts includes a long narrative comment (lines 8-20) that reiterates field meaning and caller context rather than documenting non-obvious constraints.
image-utils.ts `getAspectRatioStyle` (lines 58-60) is a one-line pass-through to another helper without added behavior.
useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx embeds an extended usage example block (lines 15-25) for a simple hook API, adding maintenance overhead with limited runtime value.
findings.suggestion: Trim comments to only non-obvious invariants, remove pass-through wrappers unless they enforce a boundary, and move usage examples into tests/docs so code files keep high signal-to-noise.

findings.summary: Root-level config files are low-fan-in pass-through shims that should be consolidated behind a single config entry pattern.
evidence: Each file is a one-line `export { default } from './config/...';` wrapper.
Holistic root-file metrics classify all five as peripheral with `fan_in` 0-1, matching the straggler signal (`fan_in < 5`).
The actual concern-specific directories already exist (`config/tailwind/`, `config/vite/`, `config/testing/`), so root placement is compatibility-only rather than ownership-based.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) choose one canonical config surface (`config/*`) and document it in project scripts/tooling; (2) move consumers to canonical paths first (`desloppify move vite.config.ts config/vite/vite.config.ts --dry-run`, `desloppify move vitest.config.ts config/testing/vitest.config.ts --dry-run`, same for edge configs and tailwind); (3) after consumer migration, delete root wrappers in one commit; (4) validate with `pnpm vitest -c config/testing/vitest.config.ts`, `pnpm vitest -c config/testing/vitest.edge.config.ts`, and `pnpm vite --config config/vite/vite.config.ts build`.

findings.summary: `src/shared/hooks` is an oversized mixed-concern directory, reducing navigability and ownership clarity.
evidence: Assigned files in this single folder span unrelated domains: auth/billing API token workflows (`useApiTokens.ts`), generic infra helper (`useAsyncOperation.ts`), and settings persistence orchestration (`useAutoSaveSettings.ts`).
Holistic directory profile reports `src/shared/hooks/` at 95 files (16,727 LOC), exceeding the >10 mixed-concern threshold by a wide margin.
Test placement (`useAutoTopup.test.ts`, `useAutoSaveSettings.test.ts`) mirrors the same catch-all layout, reinforcing a flat shared namespace.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create target folders `src/shared/hooks/async/`, `src/shared/hooks/settings/`, `src/features/account/hooks/` (or `src/features/billing/hooks/` for billing-specific hooks/tests); (2) move infra hooks first (`useAsyncOperation.ts` + test), then settings hooks (`useAutoSaveSettings.ts` + test), then domain hooks/tests (`useApiTokens.ts`, `useAutoTopup.test.ts`) into feature folders; (3) run import updates via `desloppify move <src> <dst> --dry-run` then real moves; (4) validate with `pnpm vitest src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAsyncOperation.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAutoSaveSettings.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAutoTopup.test.ts` and `pnpm tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: App-shell and feature-specific UI components are placed in `shared/components`, weakening domain-aligned top-level decomposition.
evidence: `AppErrorBoundary.tsx` describes itself as an app-level boundary for wrapping the entire app but is housed under `shared/components`.
`ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx` includes feature/domain behavior (LoRA trigger words, model-specific multi-stage fields, video preview heuristics) not broadly reusable as generic shared UI.
Tests are colocated in the same shared area, indicating the architecture currently codifies these as shared primitives.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create `src/app/components/error/` for app-shell boundaries and `src/features/lora/components/` for LoRA-specific UI; (2) move `AppErrorBoundary.tsx` + test first to app folder, then `ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx` + test to feature folder; (3) update import sites with `desloppify move src/shared/components/AppErrorBoundary.tsx src/app/components/error/AppErrorBoundary.tsx --dry-run` and equivalent move commands for tests and LoRA display; (4) validate with `pnpm vitest src/shared/components/AppErrorBoundary.test.tsx src/shared/components/ActiveLoRAsDisplay.test.tsx` (updated paths) and `pnpm tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: Core error facade acts as a global hub while encoding UI presentation contract details, increasing cross-layer coupling.
evidence: `handleError.ts` defines `ErrorPresentationPayload` with UI-facing `variant: 'destructive'` and exposes `setErrorPresenter`, so a core error utility governs presentation semantics.
The same file is a 247-importer hub per holistic context, so any contract drift affects a wide blast radius.
UI primitives live under `src/shared/components/ui/` (57 files), but presentation contract defaults originate in shared lib instead of UI boundary.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 create `src/shared/contracts/errorPresentation.ts` (payload type only) and move default presentation mapping into `src/shared/components/ui/registerToastErrorPresenter.ts`; Stage 2 keep `handleError` pure (normalize/log/notify by interface) and remove direct `variant` semantics from lib; Stage 3 migrate imports from `setErrorPresenter` to UI registration module. Move order: contract type -> UI adapter -> lib cleanup -> callsite updates. Import update/validation commands: `rg "setErrorPresenter|ErrorPresentationPayload" src`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test -- handleError button`, `npm run quality:check`.

findings.summary: Compatibility policy is documented but enforcement is inconsistent between type shims and Supabase runtime shims.
evidence: README explicitly declares strict enforcement for removed `@/types/shots` alias and names a dedicated check command.
`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` still exports deprecated `supabase` proxy with removal target `2026-06-30`, but README does not define an equivalent quality gate command for this shim.
`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` contains fallback lazy init behavior that preserves legacy path even when bootstrap contract is declared as preferred.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 add governance checks script under `scripts/contracts/` to fail CI on `import { supabase } from '@/integrations/supabase/client'`; Stage 2 update README Compatibility Shims with explicit gate command and sunset criteria; Stage 3 remove proxy export after zero-usage period and keep only `initializeSupabase/getSupabaseClient`. Move order: add check -> wire to `quality:check` -> remediate imports -> remove proxy. Import update/validation commands: `rg "import\s*\{\s*supabase\s*\}" src`, `npm run quality:check`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test -- supabase`.

findings.summary: Type ownership and utility packaging are split across broad shared buckets, reducing navigability and contract locality.
evidence: `generationAndShots.ts` is a high-fan-in barrel (205 importers) over legacy `src/types/*` while holistic structure also contains `src/domains/generation/types/`, creating two potential homes for generation contracts.
`src/shared/lib/utils.ts` is a generic name with only two exports but very high usage (101 importers), a classic package straggler/choke point.
Holistic structure shows `src/shared/lib/` has 79 files with mixed responsibilities (>10 mixed-concern threshold), indicating over-broad package boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 create target folders `src/domains/generation/contracts/` and `src/shared/lib/text/` + `src/shared/lib/ui/`; Stage 2 move generation type exports from `src/types/generationAndShots.ts` to `src/domains/generation/contracts/index.ts` and keep a temporary re-export shim; Stage 3 split `src/shared/lib/utils.ts` into explicit barrels (`uiClassNames` under `shared/lib/ui`, `stringFormatting` under `shared/lib/text`) and delete generic `utils.ts`; Stage 4 update imports and remove shims after zero-usage check. Move order: add new barrels -> dual-export transitional phase -> import rewrites -> shim removal. Import update/validation commands: `rg "@/types/generationAndShots|@/shared/lib/utils" src`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test`, `npm run quality:check`.

findings.summary: Timeline responsibilities are split across component and orchestrator, leaving two orchestration centers.
evidence: `Timeline.tsx` still owns adapter error handling, task fetch/wiring, image preloading, event subscription, and lightbox behavior (`265-533`) despite introducing dedicated timeline hooks.

`useTimelineOrchestrator.ts` simultaneously manages drag, zoom, drop, duplication, tap-to-move, endpoint drag, and structure-video selection (`141-620`), creating overlapping orchestration boundaries with `Timeline.tsx`.
findings.suggestion: Make `Timeline.tsx` a thin composition layer only. Move remaining task/lightbox/preload/event logic into focused hooks (`useTimelineLightboxData`, `useTimelineTaskDetails`, `useTimelinePreload`) and let `useTimelineOrchestrator` own only interaction state/commands.

findings.summary: Shared lightbox state abstraction has a kitchen-sink API that mixes many concerns.
evidence: `UseSharedLightboxStateProps` composes six concern-specific interfaces plus extra fields into one broad input contract (`53-138`).

`UseSharedLightboxStateReturn` returns a large nested object spanning variants, navigation, star, references, lineage, shots, source generation, make-main, effective media, layout, and button props (`144-253`).
`ImageLightbox.tsx` then adds another large environment builder (`95-193`) and forwards many pieces into shared state, indicating abstraction leakage rather than simplification.
findings.suggestion: Split `useSharedLightboxState` into narrower hooks per concern (`useLightboxVariants`, `useLightboxShotActions`, `useLightboxLayout`) and have `ImageLightbox` compose only what it needs via small typed adapters.

findings.summary: LoRA manager behavior is duplicated in two hooks with near-parallel logic.
evidence: Both hooks implement add/remove/strength handlers (`useLoraManager` `151-217`; `useLoraSync` `111-147`).
Both implement trigger-word prompt append using a latest prompt ref (`useLoraManager` `219-231`; `useLoraSync` `149-158`).
Both implement project save/load flows and expose `renderHeaderActions` with `LoraHeaderActions` (`useLoraManager` `234-427`; `useLoraSync` `161-243`).
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared, UI-agnostic LoRA domain hook/service for state transforms + persistence, then keep tool-specific wrappers only for data-shape adaptation.

findings.summary: Timeline image eligibility/filter rules are reimplemented in separate modules.
evidence: `useTimelineCore` computes positioned/unpositioned items using local filters for timeline_frame, video exclusion, and valid location (`159-183`).
`pairConfig.ts` defines `filterImageShotGenerations` with a separate but similar eligibility rule set (`24-31`) before building image and pair payloads (`34-67`, `81-89`).
findings.suggestion: Create one shared timeline-image selector utility (or typed query adapter) and reuse it in both timeline state and pair-config building to prevent semantic drift.

findings.summary: Task creation modules mix validation, payload mapping, defaults, and I/O in single files.
evidence: `imageGeneration.ts` contains mode filtering, validation, resolution/model selection, parameter synthesis, and createTask execution in one module (`46-529`).

`individualTravelSegment.ts` combines validation, coercion helpers, legacy cleanup, state derivation, payload assembly, parent/child lookup queries, resolution resolution, and createTask call (`92-709`).
findings.suggestion: Split each task module into: `validate.ts`, `deriveState.ts`, `buildPayload.ts`, and `submitTask.ts`; keep I/O boundaries thin and move pure transforms into testable pure functions.

findings.summary: Shared UI primitives diverge on code style and `cn` import source, creating a style island in a high-visibility module.
evidence: `button.tsx` uses `cn` from `@/shared/lib/utils`, while `label.tsx`/`toast.tsx`/`tooltip.tsx` use `@/shared/components/ui/cn`.
`touchableTooltip.tsx` uses single quotes + semicolons, unlike neighboring UI primitives that use double quotes + no semicolons.
findings.suggestion: Choose one canonical `cn` import path for UI primitives and enforce one formatting profile for `src/shared/components/ui/**` (formatter/lint autofix plus a brief module convention note).

findings.summary: Task and mutation flows mix normalized AppError handling with raw thrown errors and ad-hoc console logging.
evidence: Task modules typically use `handleError(error, ...); throw error;`, but `useGenerationMutations.ts` logs with `console.error` and throws `variantError` directly in one path.
This yields inconsistent downstream error shapes/context (normalized `AppError` in some paths, raw provider errors in others).
findings.suggestion: Standardize mutation/task failures on one boundary contract: always normalize/wrap before throw (or always return typed operation results), and remove direct `console.error` in favor of centralized error instrumentation.

findings.summary: Sibling `complete_task` modules use different error-context strategies, reducing predictability in logs and failure diagnostics.
evidence: `orchestrator.ts` frequently throws contextual `new Error('[OrchestratorComplete] ...')` messages around DB failures.
`generation.ts` catches, logs via `console.error`, and rethrows without equivalent contextual wrapping.
`calculate-task-cost/index.ts` primarily maps failures to structured HTTP JSON errors with logger flushes.
findings.suggestion: Adopt one edge-function error policy across task-related functions (context-rich error wrapping + structured logger fields + consistent HTTP mapping at handler boundary).

findings.summary: Client navigation prechecks use stored-token heuristics while auth actions use Supabase-validated flows, creating inconsistent authorization semantics.
evidence: `routes.tsx` redirects based on `hasStoredSessionToken()` from local storage, which checks token presence rather than validated session state.
Auth operations (`useAuthReferralFinalize`, `useDiscordSignIn`) rely on Supabase RPC/OAuth through `runAuthSupabaseOperation`, i.e., validated backend/session paths.
findings.suggestion: Use a single auth decision source for navigation (e.g., resolved session state from auth manager/context) and keep local-storage checks as non-authoritative optimization only.

findings.summary: Migration shims remain active across auth/runtime and feature entrypoints, keeping old and new access paths alive.
evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` exports deprecated `supabase` proxy with removal target 2026-06-30 while also introducing `getSupabaseClient()`/`initializeSupabase()`.
`src/features/prompt-editor/components/BulkEditControls.tsx` is an explicit migration compatibility re-export.
`src/domains/generation/hooks/useSmartPolling.ts` is a pure forwarder to shared hook; no domain-owned behavior.
`src/app/hooks/useAuthGuard.ts` still imports `supabase` proxy instead of `getSupabaseClient`.
findings.suggestion: Complete migration in phases: Stage 1 target folders `src/features/prompt-editor/components/` and `src/domains/generation/hooks/` by replacing re-export shims with direct imports at call sites; Stage 2 remove `supabase` proxy imports by codemodding to `getSupabaseClient()` in app/hooks and feature hooks; Stage 3 delete deprecated exports and shim files. Move order: consumers first, shim files second, deprecated exports last. Validate with `rg "from '@/integrations/supabase/client'.*supabase|BulkEditControls|useSmartPolling" src`, `pnpm test -w 1`, and `pnpm build`.

findings.summary: Similar async operations expose incompatible failure contracts (throw vs null/empty fallback), making callers and retries inconsistent.
evidence: `useAIInteractionService` has `*Safe` wrappers returning `null`/`[]` after catch, while `useCredits` throws explicit `Error` objects for failed reads.
Query retry classifier in `queryClient.ts` depends on message text containing 'unauthorized'/'forbidden', but auth helpers throw context strings like 'Not authenticated' and 'Failed to get session'.
This creates diverging caller expectations and retry paths for equivalent auth/network failures.
findings.suggestion: Define one app-wide async error contract per layer (data hooks throw typed errors; UI adaptors convert to toast/null once at boundary), then normalize retry classification to error codes/types instead of substring matching.

findings.summary: Supabase instrumentation relies on global monkey-patching and runtime interception, increasing hidden coupling and blast radius.
evidence: `installRealtimeInstrumentation` overrides `console.warn` globally and uses dynamic import side effects to trigger reconnect behavior.
`installWindowOnlyInstrumentation` overrides `window.WebSocket`, `window.onerror`, and `window.onunhandledrejection` globally.
These effects are installed during shared runtime initialization, so unrelated modules are impacted by instrumentation policy.
findings.suggestion: Isolate instrumentation into opt-in adapters: move global patching behind explicit debug-only bootstrap flags and replace console interception with direct event hooks from owned reconnect/auth modules.

findings.summary: Service hook API is inflated by parallel `OrThrow`/normal/`Safe` variants that mostly forward behavior.
evidence: `useAIInteractionService` exports 9 operation variants for 3 core actions, with substantial duplication and thin wrappers around shared invokes.
Adjacent preload orchestration similarly wraps one coordinator call with minimal additional logic, indicating repeated indirection style.
findings.suggestion: Collapse to one canonical method per action plus one boundary adapter (UI-safe wrapper) in consuming layer; keep throw semantics in service layer and remove duplicated wrapper methods.

findings.summary: Low-fan-in bridge files remain in feature/domain folders even though implementation ownership lives in shared packages.
evidence: Objective structure: `src/domains/generation/hooks/` has `avg_fan_in=0.0`, `total_loc=4`, dominated by forwarding exports rather than owned module logic.
Both listed files are pure re-exports to `src/shared/*`, a direct import-affinity mismatch (>60% external ownership by construction).
This creates extra navigation hops and weakens package-by-ownership boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 target folders `src/shared/hooks/` and `src/shared/components/` as canonical ownership, and update all imports to point there directly; Stage 2 delete bridge files in `src/domains/generation/hooks/` and `src/features/prompt-editor/components/`; Stage 3 keep only domain-owned files under those folders (or remove folder if empty). Move order: consumer import updates -> barrel cleanup -> file deletion. Run `rg "domains/generation/hooks/useSmartPolling|features/prompt-editor/components/BulkEditControls" src`, then `pnpm test -w 1`, then `pnpm build` to validate import graph and bundling.
